scenario.title: Learning workbench — URL as state
scenario.question: Workbench: URL-as-state, session 2
scenario.mission: Understand the URL-as-state pattern well enough to design the next filterable surface without re-deriving it: where state lives, why the core stays pure, and what history traversal does to both.
scenario.practiceLede: Not trivia — each question is a call you would make while designing or debugging a URL-driven surface. Misses map back to their waypoint.

lessons.title: The URL is the only state
body: The tag filter keeps zero state outside the address bar: no store, no cache, no persistence. Whatever question the page must answer — what is filtered, what should render, what does back mean — the answer is re-derived from location.search.
This is why sharing a link reproduces exactly what the sender saw, and why the Gate A non-goal (no persistence) was load-bearing rather than a limitation.
lessons.flowCaption: One direction: the view derives from the URL; nothing flows back except navigation.

facts.text: Sharing a URL reproduces the exact view — state travels with the link (feature-xray record).
Persistence was an explicit Gate A non-goal, which is what keeps back/forward trivially correct (spec.md).

lessons.title: Pure core, effectful edge
body: Two pure functions carry the whole behavior: parseTagFromSearch turns a URL into intent, filterByTag turns intent into a visible list. Both run identically in Node tests and the browser.
Only the outermost edge — replaceChildren — touches the DOM. Keeping mutation at one edge is what makes the render path testable and the invariant simple: same URL, same render, any entry route.
lessons.flowCaption: Purity gradient, left to right: everything testable until the final edge.

facts.text: node --test imports the same functions the browser runs — no shim, no mock DOM (feature-xray record).

lessons.title: History traversal is not a load
body: Back and forward re-run the same render path via popstate — usually. Safari's back-forward cache can restore the whole page image without firing popstate at all, which is exactly how stale rows survived under a fresh URL.
The durable habit: treat pageshow with persisted=true as a first-class entry route, alongside load and popstate. Any URL-driven surface that forgets one entry route will eventually render a lie.
lessons.flowCaption: Three entry routes into one render path; the dashed one is the diagnosed gap.

facts.text: bfcache restores showed pageshow.persisted=true with no popstate, in exact correlation with the stale rows (find-the-cause record).
pageshow fires on every restore from the back/forward cache; popstate only fires on history traversal within a live page (MDN).

questions.prompt: Where does the filter state live between two visits?

options.label: In a small store synced to the URL
In browser storage keyed by session

questions.explanation: The pattern's whole contract: any view is derivable from the address bar alone, which is what makes shared links exact.
questions.gap: 01 · The URL is the only state

questions.prompt: Why do the two core functions stay free of DOM access?

options.label: So the identical code runs in Node tests and the browser
Because modules cannot import the DOM

questions.explanation: Purity is the testing seam: the same imports run under node --test and in app.mjs, so behavior is proven without a browser.

questions.prompt: A user presses back and the page shows the old list under the new URL. First suspect?

options.label: The filter function returned stale data
A render race between two handlers
A bfcache restore that fired no popstate

questions.explanation: Cache restores resurrect the old DOM without re-running the glue — the diagnosed cause, and the entry route designs forget first.
questions.gap: 03 · History traversal is not a load

questions.prompt: You are adding a second filterable surface. What is the first design commitment?

options.label: Choose the state container and sync strategy
Name the URL schema and derive everything from it

questions.explanation: The pattern generalizes by starting from the address bar: one schema, pure derivation, one effectful edge, all entry routes owned.
questions.gap: 01 · The URL is the only state

unresolved: session 3 candidate topic: applying the pattern to multi-parameter state

accepted.acceptedVia: chat 2026-07-23 — session export pasted and confirmed after retrieval practice
accepted.summary: Second session on the URL-as-state pattern: lessons on single-source state, the purity gradient, and history entry routes; retrieval practice scored 3/4 with the bfcache entry route as the waypoint to review next session.

decisions.rationale: The miss was q3 — I still reach for a render race before remembering restore semantics.

exampleWorking.reflection: The entry-routes framing finally made bfcache feel structural instead of trivia; the miss confirms it needs one more pass.

accepted.canonicalBody:
# URL-as-state — session 2

Score 3/4. Review next session: waypoint 03 (history traversal is not a
load — bfcache restores fire pageshow, not popstate). Sessions remain
diagnostic learning state; nothing here gates anything.
